observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to:

a.	exert effort to retrieve the disbursement vouchers and the supporting documents of the recorded payables aged two years and more to identify the valid claimants; and




b.	send confirmation letters for balances with identified claimants or creditors. Valid claims shall be paid. Otherwise, cause the reversion of the payables to the Accumulated Surplus of the GF and furnish copies of the Journal Entry Voucher to the Municipal Budget Officer.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2023 Page 47 | The Municipal Accountant has already processed the payment of some transactions retrieved from the end-user representative.
observations.reason:
Some disbursement vouchers and the supporting documents of the recorded payables were not yet retrieved.

Implemented

Retrieved disbursement vouchers have valid claims. A total of ₱230,358.30 were paid in CY 2024.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

a.	direct the GSO Designate in coordination with the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to finalize the Inspection and Inventory Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) with provision of reasonable appraisal value of the unserviceable properties and promptly cause the disposal of these properties; and

b.	advise the personnel concerned to submit the IIRUP to the Accounting Office upon completion of the disposal activity as it will be the basis for adjusting the balances of the affected PPE accounts.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2023 Page 49 | The GSO-Designate have already identified the unserviceable properties of the Municipality; for assessment and validation of the committee on disposal before sending request to the Commission.
observations.reason:
Only the IIRUP for motor vehicles and construction and heavy equipment was prepared by the GSO and the Committee. 


Not Implemented

No disposal had been made because the unserviceable properties are still subject to inspections.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the barangays to immediately liquidate the fund transfers and instruct those with difficulty of retrieving the documents for liquidation to coordinate with and request copies of documents from concerned agencies/entities to comply with the submission of the required liquidation documents.

Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant monitor and verify the records of unliquidated fund transfers prior to any grant to ensure that no additional funds be released to IAs with unliquidated balances.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2023 Page 53 | The Municipal Accountant sent demand letters to concerned barangays for the submission of documents for the liquidation of funds.
observations.reason:
Only two out of eight barangays have submitted their liquidation reports.



Implemented

The Municipal Accountant already monitors and verifies the records of unliquidated fund transfers before granting additional funds to IAs.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Assessor to prioritize the titling of the 20 parcels of land and/or register under the Torrens Title System by appropriating funds in its proposed budget to facilitate titling of these parcels of land.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2023 Page 54 | The Municipal Assessor submitted and resubmitted documents for the Special Patent Applications of the Municipality real properties to the Department of Environment and Natural Resources.
observations.reason: The Municipal Assessor is still securing other documentary requirements as required by the agency to proceed with the application.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Finance Committee make representation through the Local Chief Executive to the Sangguniang Bayan for the revision and update of the Local Revenue Code of the Municipality to maximize its revenue raising ability for the delivery of basic services beneficial to its constituents.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2023 Page 55 | Officials and employees have attended Seminar-Workshop on Updating the Local Revenue Code on April 16-19, 2024, to be capacitated on the updating of the Local Revenue Code of the Municipality. The Management also issued Memorandum No. RRLBII 2024-09-87 dated September 17,2024 in relation to Local Revenue Code updating using the Local Fees and Charges (LFC) Toolkit.
observations.reason: Revision and update of the Local Revenue Code of the Municipality has not been made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management prepare the necessary feasibility studies and a detailed business plan for the operations of the LEE and the FSSE report at the end of each 5-year period to properly monitor the efficiency and effectiveness of the operations and decide whether it will continue or discontinue operating as a LEE.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2023 Page 59 | The LCE issued EO No. 41 - Creating and Composing a Technical Working Group to formulate, prepare feasibility studies and detailed business plans and conduct financial self-sufficiency evaluations for the economic enterprises of the municipality.
observations.reason: The necessary feasibility study and detailed business plan for the operations of the LEE and the corresponding FSSE report were not yet submitted to the Audit Team for review.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management comply with the requirements of localizing the Safe Spaces Act of 2019 to adopt measures localizing the applicability and ensure enforcement of the law, to further promote the general welfare of the people.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2022 Page 45 | The Sangguniang Bayan of Pagudpud enacted Ordinance No. 2023-071 for this purpose and submitted it to the Sangguniang Panlalawigan (SP) for review.
observations.reason: No update of the SP on the submitted revised ordinance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management instruct the Municipal Social Welfare and Development (MSWD)-livelihood focal point and barangay officials to make every effort to collect the outstanding loans so that they will return to the Municipality’s coffers and be used to fund important projects and programs for the benefit of its constituents.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2022 Page 48 | The MSWDO coordinated with the barangay officials to remind the recipients of the payment of their loan balances.
observations.reason: Total collections made for CY 2024 were ₱64,990.00 with balance totaling ₱261,625.00 as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer to collect and update the lease agreements with the representatives of the interred individuals in order to raise additional funds for the Municipality.
observations.managementAction:
AAR
2020
Page
38 | The Management has installed a tarpaulin within the vicinity of the cemetery to remind the representatives of the interred individuals to update and pay the corresponding fees for the spaces rented in the cemetery
observations.reason: The Municipal Treasurer have collected a total of ₱37,500.00 for CY 2024. However, the lease agreements have not yet been updated.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management intensify and enforce the collection of these overdue receivables. 


We further recommended that management conduct analysis and verifications of the receivables and, if all possible means to collect have been exhausted and the same proved futile, request for write-off the accounts following the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.
observations.managementAction:
AAR
2019
Page 37 | The management have exhausted all possible means of retrieving the records to identify the recipients
observations.reason:
No collections were made throughout the year for the loan amount.

Not Implemented

No records were retrieved to identify the recipients of the loans. Officials/ employees during the time of grant were no longer in service.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management instruct the:
Municipal Treasurer to submit to the Municipal Accountant an updated and complete duly certified list of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for at year end and every year thereafter; and
Municipal Accountant to set up the RPT and SET Receivables for each year corresponding to the lists and make the necessary adjusting entries as warranted and observe the prescribed accounting procedures in setting up the RPT and SET receivable and deferred income accounts.

We recommended that the Management strictly impose the compliance of the OSHS in accordance with RA No. 11058 upon their contractors, otherwise no payment is required on the allocated cost for it.
Also, require the contractors to refund the unimplemented OSHP totaling to ₱1.391 million.
We recommended that the Management instruct the municipal officials and employees to follow the limits set under EO No. 77 on the charges of DTE.

We recommended that Management require the GSO Designate to update his records and prepare a property transfer report and for Municipal Accountant to derecognize the properties issued to public schools upon transfer.
observations.managementAction:
AAR 2023 Page 48 | The Municipal Accountant has already derecognized in the books the asset accounts based on the Deed and Donations and Certificate of Turnover and Acceptance given by the GSO and Municipal Engineer.
However, the Machinery, Equipment, Furniture and Fixtures accounts for the National Child Development Center will be retained in books since custody of these properties is with the MSWD Officer, a permanent employee.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant prepared adjusting entries through JEV Nos. 200-2024-06-173, 200-2024-09-254 and 200-2024-12-369.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the implementing offices for the submission of the complete distribution lists and thereafter to make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances of the inventory, expense, and receivable accounts.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2023 Page 51 | The Municipal Accountant adjusted the corresponding balances of the accounts. The Municipal Accountant also instructed the Municipal Agriculturist to provide the office monthly inventory and collection reports.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant adjusted the corresponding balances of the accounts upon submission of the complete distribution lists through JEV Nos. 100-2024-05-2591 and 100-2024-05-2592 dated May 31,2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the Supply Officer/GSO Designate to apply to the Bureau of Treasury a bond sufficient to cover his accountability on the properties of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2023 Page 56 | Fund for this purpose was already included in the approved 2025 Annual Budget and the GSO Designate have already prepared the initial requirements for him to apply bond immediately on the 1st month of the budgeted year.
observations.reason: The Supply Officer/GSO Designate is already bonded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management require the BAC to submit to the Auditor’s Office all perfected contracts and purchase orders together with all its supporting documents irrespective of the amount within five working days from the date of its execution/approval in compliance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 for the timely review and verification of the transactions and immediately validate the terms and conditions stipulated therein.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2023 Page 57 | The BAC submitted to the Auditor’s Office all perfected contracts and purchase orders together with all its supporting documents irrespective of the amount through email for transactions from the date of the AOM to present.
observations.reason: Perfected contracts and purchase orders together with all their supporting documents were submitted through email.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management require the BAC to comply with the posting requirements to the PhilGEPS on procurements conducted through the alternative modes of procurement with amount above ₱50,000.00.



Also, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that the printout of the PhilGEPS postings of the Invitations or Requests for submission of price quotations/proposals, NOA and NTP together with the approved contract or purchase order be among the supporting documents for the payment of procured goods.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2023 Page 58 | Procurements conducted from April 2024 to present through the alternative modes of procurement with amount above ₱50,000.00 are posted to the PhilGEPS (Invitations, NOA, NTP) and are required as attachments to DVs together with the Contracts/ Purchase Orders upon payment.
observations.reason:
BAC have already complied with the posting requirements to the PhilGEPS on procurements conducted through the alternative modes of procurement with amount above ₱50,000.00 for CY 2024.

Implemented

Printout of the PhilGEPS postings of the Invitations or Requests for submission of price quotations/proposals, NOA and NTP of procurements above ₱50,000.00 were already attached to the disbursement vouchers.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:

a.	instruct the Acceptance and Inspection Committee to properly inspect all succeeding deliveries if it conforms with the required specifications in the approved Purchase Order before acceptance, and 


b.	exert efforts to recover the amount equivalent to the deficiency of the engine size.
observations.managementAction: AAR 2023 Page 61 | The GSO-Designate sent Quotations to suppliers for the vehicle with the required specifications to determine the variance to be collected. Only 1 quotation was received, and the amount was higher than the purchase cost.
observations.reason:
The Acceptance and Inspection Committee already inspect properly the specifications of succeeding deliveries. 

Not Implemented

The amount equivalent of deficiency of the engine size of the vehicles has not yet been recovered.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to:
exert effort to retrieve the disbursement vouchers and the supporting documents of the recorded payables aged two years and more in order to identify the valid claimants; and
send confirmation letters for balances with identified claimants or creditors. Valid claims shall be paid. Otherwise, cause the reversion of the payables to the Accumulated Surplus of the GF and furnish copies of the Journal Entry Voucher to the Municipal Budget Officer.

We recommended that Management require the GSO Designate to update his records and prepare a property transfer report and for Municipal Accountant to derecognize the properties issued to public schools upon transfer.

We recommended that Management:
direct the GSO Designate in coordination with the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to finalize the Inspection and Inventory Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) with provision of reasonable appraisal value of the unserviceable properties and promptly cause the disposal of these properties; and
advise the concerned personnel to submit the IIRUP to the Accounting Office upon completion of the disposal activity as it will be the basis for adjusting the balances of the affected PPE accounts.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the implementing offices for the submission of the complete distribution lists and thereafter to make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the balances of the inventory, expense, and receivable accounts.

We recommended the management to instruct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to capitalize the interest expenses incurred during the construction of the new public market as part of the cost of the assets amounting to ₱12,557,007.37 to properly reflect the correct balance of the PPE and GE accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Management have adjusted the amount in the books through Journal Entry.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management to require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to submit succeeding Trial Balance and Financial Statements for review of the Audit Team in accordance with Section 41 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Management have fast tracked the needed reports monthly to be able to meet the set deadline for the Year-End Reporting.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the management to require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to submit all the journals, DVs, payroll, and other financial transaction documents of the Municipality for succeeding audit periods within the period required under Section 7.2.1(a) of the RRSA - COA Circular No. 2009-006, to give the Auditor an ample time to evaluate the validity, legality, and propriety of the municipal transactions.
observations.managementAction: The Management has fast tracked the needed reports monthly to be able to meet the set deadline for the submission of journals, DVs, payrolls, and other financial transaction documents of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the management to instruct the GSO-designate to remove the motto displayed on the vehicles distributed to the different barangays. 

We further recommended the management not to accept deliveries that bear any marks that are associated with the Municipality’s leadership to prevent display of unnecessary expenses to government properties.
observations.managementAction: The Management already removed the motto displayed as per recommendation and the COA further inspected for validation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the Management instruct the Human Resources and Management Officer (HRMO) to strictly abide with the provisions set-forth to allow monetization of sick leave credits if and only if the employee applying for monetization has exhausted his/her vacation leave credits within the allowable limits.

Furthermore, we recommended the HRMO restate the vacation and sick leave credits of the employees who availed the monetization of both vacation and sick leave to properly reflect the correct balances of their leave credits in each period.
observations.managementAction: The HRMO have already restated the VL and SL leave credits of the employees concerned.

We recommended that Management require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to be guided with the different periods in order not to incur delay and the benefits derived from government projects will be enjoyed the soonest possible time.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to immediately renew all expired fidelity bond of officers required to be bonded in order to strengthen the protection of government funds and properties from possible losses, as well as to avoid any administrative liability that may be imposed against erring officers and employees.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has renewed the fidelity bond of the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Vice- Mayor on June 29, 2021. Confirmation Letter No. 2021-495 from the Bureau of the Treasury dated June 29, 2021.
observations.reason: Concerned Municipal Officials were bonded as per Bond Confirmation Letter No. 2021-495 from the Bureau of the Treasury dated June 29, 2021.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, whenever applicable, implement the programs, projects and activities embodied in the Economic Development Plan. We also recommended that management revisit the plans and budget embodied in the municipality’s Economic Development Fund and identify the necessities of the listed projects therein, otherwise identify a more appropriate and responsive programs. Moreover, we recommended that the municipality appropriate the savings of funds derived from previous implementation of projects to new projects that are aligned to the purpose of the fund. These will ensure that benefits intended to for the agency’s populace will be met.
observations.managementAction: Subject projects were implemented in 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the General Services Officer–Designate to prepare the Inventory and Inspection on Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) of the identified unserviceable properties which will serve as the basis in the disposal and dropping from the municipality’s book of accounts in order to properly reflect the accurate value of Property, Plant and Equipment in the financial statements. Further, we recommended the management to follow the procedures in the proper disposal of properties.

We recommended the Municipal Accountant to stop the practice of paying PERA to casual employees on a fixed monthly rate of P2,000.00, instead use the rate of P90.91 per day as required under Item 5.5 of DBM Budget Circular No. 2009-3 dated August 18, 2009 to avoid disallowance in audit.

We recommended the OIC-Municipal Engineer to stop using the 12% for the computation of VAT component of ABCs and to strictly abide to the provisions of DPWH D.O. No. 22 dated February 18, 2015 for proper and reasonable computation of the same which may result to disallowance in audit.

We recommended the Municipal Treasurer to direct Ms. Rochelle L. Garvida to present the four (4) pieces of BIR Form 0016 (CTC-Individual) with inclusive serial numbers of 04065397 to 04065400, to properly account Ms. Garvida’s accountabilities.
We recommended management to transfer/convert the Savings Account to a Current Account to strengthen the internal control over cash, and reconcile with bank of the noted discrepancy of P1,593,616.03.

We recommended management to require the Municipal Accountant and MDRRM Officer to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRM Fund pursuant to COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 in order to properly monitor and evaluate whether the utilization is within the projects, programs and activities identified.

We recommended management to fully utilize the 20% component of IRA for development projects in order to address the priority needs of the municipality’s citizens and achieve the benefits intended therefrom.

We have recommended the General Services Officer to prepare necessary Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Properties of the identified unserviceable properties which will be the basis in the disposal of the properties and its derecognition from the books in order to reflect the correct value of Property, Plant and Equipment in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
Adjustment was made for the non-existing building. With regard to the unserviceable pro-perties, the LCE had issued an order constituting the Bids and Awards Committee on Disposal tasked to conduct disposal activities of un-serviceable pro-perties. Preliminary activities were also conducted in various offices to initiate the disposal.

Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the agency should not peg the Subsistence Allowance to P1,500.00 but rather compute the allowance on a per day basis as required under existing rules and regulations. We likewise recommended that an employee must have a written authority from the LCE before he/she could avail of subsistence allowance for “on-call” days. We further recommended that the agency should implement a stronger control in its payroll system in order to avoid situations where employees are paid for days that they are not allowed to receive Subsistence and Laundry Allowance.
observations.managementAction: The agency has already computing the Subsistence Allowance on a per day basis. Also, a written authority is issued whenever an employee works on “on-call” basis. The H.R. also attaches in the payroll a certification for the actual number of days the PHWs and Social Workers reported to their official station.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the agency that 20% Development Fund be utilized during the year to address the priority needs of the people and to attain maximum benefits for the intended recipients.
We have recommended the Agency to furnish the Auditor a copy of contract and its supporting documents within five (5) days upon execution for review.
observations.managementAction: The agency now furnishes the Office of the Auditor copies of perfected contracts entered by the LGU.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the General Services Officer to prepare the necessary Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Properties of the identified unserviceable properties which will be the basis in the disposal of the properties and its derecognition from the books in order to reflect the correct value of Property, Plant and Equipment in the financial statements.
We have recommended the agency to reconcile with the bank and identify the possible reason/s of the discrepancies.

We have recommended the agency should not peg the Subsistence Allowance to P1,500.00 but rather compute the allowance on a per day basis as required under existing rules and regulations. We likewise, recommend that an employee must have a written authority from the LCE before he/she could avail of subsistence allowance for “on-call” days. We further recommend that the agency should implement a stronger control in its payroll system in order to avoid situations where employees are paid for days that they are not allowed to receive Subsistence and Laundry Allowance.
We have recommended the agency that 20% Development Fund be utilized during the year to address the priority needs of the people and to attain maximum benefits for the intended recipients.
We have recommended the Agency to restate the value of the vehicle in the book of accounts and recognize its corresponding depreciation.
observations.managementAction: Complied
observations.reason: Adjustment was made to reflect the subject property

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the General Services Officer to prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Properties of the identified unserviceable properties which will be the basis in the disposal of the properties and its derecognition from the books in order to reflect the correct value of Property, Plant and Equipment in the financial statements. We further recommended the agency to refrain from disposing properties without prior inspection report from the Commission and/or other government agencies.
observations.managementAction: Partally complied
observations.reason: Adjustment was made for the non-existing building. With regard to the unserviceable properties, the LCE had issued an order constituting the Bids and Awards Committee on Disposal tasked to conduct disposal activities of unserviceable properties. Preliminary activities were also conducted in various offices to initiate the disposal..

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Municipality to strictly adhere with the Section 77 of the Local Government Code, review the contracts entered into with Job Orders and make the necessary corrections.
observations.managementAction: Complied
observations.reason: Subsequent contracts were already entered into by the LCE

observations.recommendation: We have recommended management to strictly enforce COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997 on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances. We further recommended management to cause or order the withholding of any money claims against accountable officers concerned until they have fully settled their cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Complied

observations.recommendation: We have recommended the Agency to furnish the Auditor a copy of contract and its supporting documents within five (5) days upon execution for review.
observations.managementAction: Not Complied
